QF.id: IV Strategies Using Interest Rate Derivatives_53

question.text: Under SEBI regulations, which entities are permitted to participate in exchange-traded interest rate derivatives in India?

answer.text: All persons resident in India including individuals, institutions, FPIs, and mutual funds, subject to applicable limits
Only scheduled commercial banks and primary dealers
Only FIIs and mutual funds with minimum AUM of Rs. 500 crore

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI allows broad participation in ETIRD. Residents including individuals, corporates, banks, mutual funds, insurance companies, and FPIs can participate subject to position limits and other regulations. **The Trap:** Banks and PDs were early participants but the market is open to all. There is no minimum AUM requirement. Portfolio managers can participate but are not the only ones. **Cross-Exam Context:** Participation eligibility is also tested in Series I (currency derivatives) with similar broad access. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The wide participant base was a key regulatory goal to build liquidity in the IRD market.
